title: Executive Order 13047—Prohibiting New Investment in Burma

flags.justification: Exercises authority delegated by a specific congressional statute (Section 570 of the 1997 appropriations act) requiring a specific presidential certification as a precondition, though broad IEEPA-based implementation discretion follows once that trigger is met.
No independent review process for affected investors is specified in this order's own text.
Multiple related provisions (investment prohibition, facilitation ban, evasion ban) all coherently serve one purpose.
An extensive, precise definitions section (Sec. 4) defines 'new investment' and related terms with specific exclusions for not-for-profit humanitarian activities.
No incentive structure rewarding behavior contrary to the order's purpose is identifiable.
No review or reauthorization mechanism is specified for the underlying emergency and sanctions authority.
The order is explicitly transmitted to Congress.
No external check is specified on the Secretary of the Treasury's implementation authority.
A significant sanctions action responding to a documented human-rights crisis, implementing a specific congressional directive with real international significance.
Sec. 4(f) explicitly excludes not-for-profit educational, health, and humanitarian programs from the covered 'economic development' definition — a narrow, purpose-consistent carve-out.
